step_by_step_thinking:
Radiation causes cell death primarily through the generation of free radicals, which in turn disrupt the stability of macromolecules including DNA. Additionally, radiation can directly damage DNA and induce the expression of certain genes responsible for initiating the apoptotic pathway. Among the listed options, option C (Ionization) best captures the mechanism whereby radiation induces cellular damage leading to cell death.
\boxed{C} Based on the detailed evidence provided, we see that radiation-induced cell death involves both direct DNA damage and the creation of reactive species. Option C (Ionization) encapsulates the fundamental interaction wherein ionizing radiation excites atoms/molecules, leading to secondary damaging events like free radical formation.
